risk_specification.name: Business Risk Specification
risk_specification.description: Rules to assess business risks based on various attributes.

rules.title: Phishing Attack on Active Email Account
rules.description: A phishing email is sent to an active user email account. The attack attempts to trick the user into entering credentials. Various outcomes are possible depending on the user's response.
preconditions: User has active email account
External emails not fully filtered
postconditions: Attacker may gain access to user credentials or trigger alerts

outcomes.response: User clicked link and entered credentials
User clicked link but did not enter credentials
User ignored the email
User reported the phishing attempt to sysadmin

rules.title: Privilege Escalation via Writable Sensitive File
rules.description: A compromised user account has write access to a sensitive file (e.g., sudoers file, shell profile, or cron script). The attacker can exploit this by modifying the file to escalate privileges.
preconditions: User account is compromised
Sensitive file is writable by the user
postconditions: Attacker may gain elevated privileges or trigger alerts

attack_rate.scope: per user account with writable sensitive file

outcomes.response: Attacker successfully modifies file to escalate privileges
Attack attempt triggers security alert (e.g., file integrity monitoring)
Sysadmin detects and revokes file permissions before attack succeeds

rules.title: Credential Dumping from Compromised Host
rules.description: An attacker uses tools to extract cached credentials from memory or files on a compromised machine.
preconditions: Host must be compromised
Admin credentials cached or stored locally

attack_rate.scope: per compromised host with stored admin credentials

outcomes.response: Attacker successfully dumped admin credentials
Partial credentials were obtained
Credential dumping attempt failed silently
Endpoint security detected the attempt

rules.title: Remote Service Exploitation (RDP)
rules.description: Attacker uses exposed RDP service to gain unauthorized access to internal systems using weak credentials.
preconditions: RDP port open to external network
Weak credentials in use
postconditions: The attacker gains control of the host that runs the exploited service or triggers security alerts

outcomes.response: Attacker logged in via RDP and gained control
Multiple failed RDP login attempts, security alert triggered
RDP login attempt failed without detection

rules.title: Lateral Movement via Pass-the-Hash
rules.description: Attacker reuses stolen NTLM hashes to authenticate and move laterally across hosts in the organization.
preconditions: Hashes harvested from compromised host
Target host accepts NTLM authentication
postconditions: Attacker gains unauthorized access to other systems or generates alerts

outcomes.response: Attacker successfully authenticated using NTLM hash and compromised host
Suspicious login attempt detected and flagged
Authentication attempt failed and remained undetected

rules.title: Data Exfiltration via Cloud Storage
rules.description: Attacker uses compromised credentials to exfiltrate sensitive data to unauthorized cloud services.
preconditions: Attacker has access to sensitive files
Cloud storage is reachable from internal network
postconditions: Data confidentiality compromised or detected and blocked

outcomes.response: Full exfiltration of sensitive data via cloud completed
Partial data exfiltration occurred and suspicious activity was logged
Data loss prevention system blocked exfiltration attempt
Exfiltration attempt succeeded without detection

rules.title: Abuse of Trusted Relationships for Lateral Access
rules.description: Attacker exploits inter-host trust relationships (e.g., shared keys, unmanaged trust paths) to move laterally.
preconditions: Trusted connection exists between two hosts
One of the hosts is compromised
postconditions: Attacker gains access to a second host or triggers detection

attack_rate.scope: per trusted path from a compromised host

outcomes.response: Attacker moved laterally through trusted connection and compromised target host
Suspicious activity detected during lateral movement attempt
Movement failed due to invalid configuration or timeout

rules.title: Malicious Use of Remote Admin Tools
rules.description: An attacker uses legitimate admin tools (e.g., PsExec, WMI) to execute commands or move laterally.
preconditions: Attacker has admin credentials or remote access
Target host allows admin tool execution
postconditions: Attacker executes remote commands or causes alert

attack_rate.scope: per valid admin credential and remote execution access

outcomes.response: Attacker executed command remotely and compromised the host
Command execution blocked or logged by EDR
Attempt failed with no observable impact

rules.title: Exploitation of Public-Facing Application
rules.description: Attacker exploits a vulnerability in a public-facing application (e.g., web server or API) to gain remote access.
preconditions: Public-facing application with known vulnerability
postconditions: Application or underlying system becomes compromised

outcomes.response: Attacker successfully exploited vulnerability and accessed the system
Attack attempt detected and blocked by WAF
Exploitation failed without detection

rules.title: Unauthorized Script Execution on Server
rules.description: A malicious script (e.g., shell or PowerShell) is deployed and executed on a server due to misconfigured permissions.
preconditions: Server allows script execution
Attacker has write access or deploy permissions
postconditions: Attacker gains foothold or elevates privileges

attack_rate.scope: per exposed server with deployable script

outcomes.response: Script executed and attacker gained remote shell
Script flagged and removed by antivirus/EDR
Execution failed due to permissions or syntax error

rules.title: Exploitation via Malicious USB Device
rules.description: A malicious USB device is inserted into an internal host, executing payloads automatically (USB drop attack).
preconditions: Physical access to device
OS auto-executes USB payloads or user enables it
postconditions: Host becomes compromised or malware is neutralized

outcomes.response: Payload executed and host was compromised
Antivirus detected and blocked payload execution
Device was removed before execution

rules.title: DNS Tunneling for Covert Communication
rules.description: An attacker uses DNS requests to tunnel data or establish C2 channels without being detected by firewalls.
preconditions: Attacker controls domain name and DNS server
Target allows outbound DNS traffic
postconditions: Attacker maintains persistence or exfiltrates data

attack_rate.scope: per compromised host with DNS access

outcomes.response: Data exfiltrated or command channel established via DNS tunnel
DNS tunneling flagged by IDS
DNS tunneling failed silently

rules.title: Compromise via SaaS Credential Stuffing
rules.description: Credential stuffing attack targets cloud-based services using leaked employee credentials.
preconditions: Reused credentials leaked or stolen
SaaS services lack MFA or rate limiting
postconditions: SaaS session hijacked or login alerts triggered

outcomes.response: Attacker gained access to SaaS account and exfiltrated data
Suspicious login detected by SaaS provider and flagged
All login attempts failed with no detection

rules.title: Insider Copying Confidential Files
rules.description: A malicious or careless insider copies high-sensitivity documents to removable storage or external cloud.
preconditions: Insider has access to sensitive files
External copy method is allowed or not monitored
postconditions: Files are exfiltrated, detected, or blocked

outcomes.response: Files successfully copied and exfiltrated
File movement logged and alert generated
Copy attempt blocked by security controls

rules.title: Persistence via Scheduled Task Creation
rules.description: Attacker creates a scheduled task or cron job on a compromised host to maintain persistence.
preconditions: Host is compromised
Attacker has sufficient privileges to create scheduled tasks

outcomes.response: Scheduled task created, persistence achieved
Task creation detected by security monitoring
Task creation failed

rules.title: Defense Evasion by Disabling Security Tools
rules.description: Attacker disables antivirus or endpoint detection tools on a compromised host to avoid detection.
preconditions: Host is compromised
Security tools are installed and running
postconditions: Security controls are disabled or attempt is detected

attack_rate.scope: per compromised host with security tools

outcomes.response: Security tool disabled, host unprotected
Disabling attempt detected and blocked
Attempt failed with no impact

rules.title: Persistence via Registry Run Keys
rules.description: Attacker adds a malicious entry to Windows Registry Run keys for persistence.
preconditions: Host is Windows
Attacker has write access to registry

outcomes.response: Malicious Run key added, persistence achieved
Registry modification detected by EDR
Modification failed
